char_1775151236257.backstory:
Wendy trained in the way of the Kolari in the fortress town of Foarn. While training there she grew attached to her master Monk Duku. Their relationship stemmed from their loner nature, both of them being the quiet type and preferring to blend into the background and avoid notice. Monk Duku saw this as a strength and trained her to be stealthy and to use this to her advantage in life. He proved to be like a father to Wendy, who had lost both of her sailor parents at sea. But he always had an air of mystery about him. He was clearly not from the town of Foarn and often received suspicious courier mail and visits from dark strangers. On one occasion Wendy was able to intercept a courier and steal a single letter. It featured a drawing of a beautiful restaurant by the sea, signed with a monogram of a lizard seemingly in the shape of the letter S. Briefly after that Monk Duku disappeared without a trace, leaving behind only his large belt with a similar monogram of a lizard, this one in the shape of an H.

After Duku’s departure the new Monk in charge was Monk Wando, who came from the upscale town of Dagend. Monk Wando was a pretentious gnome, he held other students in much higher esteem than Wendy and seemed to ignore her altogether. Her feelings about the Kolari soured.

One day while Wendy was keeping watch she noticed a small party of Brevar approaching Foarn. In her hubris and frankly because she didn’t want to interact with anyone she chose to take on the party herself. Quickly she realized she was outmatched and outnumbered as this party proved to be a full scale raid, which was unprecedented in Foarn. Managing to sound the alarm bell, all but too late, Wendy dreads the memories of the many Kolari pupils that laid their life down to protect her hometown. Among them her cousin and good friend Quill. While the attack was warded off the town of Foarn would never be the same. Wendy primarily was embittered against Monk Wando who refused to help the town and pupils and chose instead to remain north of the town protecting the Kolari temple. Had the Monk in charge been in town many lives could have been spared. The town of Foarn now is a constant reminder of Wendy’s failure and guilt. 

Armed with her lethal fists and quarter staff Wendy has decided to run away from her hometown. Her goal: Find that restaurant by the sea, and discover where her beloved Master might have gone.

In her off time Wendy tinkers with little gizmos and gadgets, typically trying to invent new light weaponry to enhance her own attacks. For instance forming caltrops or throwing stars out of little gears.
char_1775151236257.name: Wendy Warmwind

proficiencies.tools: Tinker's Tools, Thieves' Tools, Disguise Kit, Flute,
proficiencies.skills: Acrobatics, Insight, Stealth, Sleight of Hand,

features.name: Rock Gnome Trait: Darkvision
features.desc: You have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You cannot discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

features.name: Rock Gnome Trait: Gnome Cunning
features.desc: You have advantage on all Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws against magic.

Whenever you make an Intelligence (History) check related to magic items, alchemical objects, or technological devices, you can add twice your proficiency bonus, instead of any proficiency bonus you normally apply.
features.name: Rock Gnome Trait: Artificer's Lore
Rock Gnome Trait: Tinker
features.desc:
You have proficiency with artisan's tools (tinker's tools). Using those tools, you can spend 1 hour and 10 gp worth of materials to construct a Tiny clockwork device (AC 5, 1 hp). The device ceases to function after 24 hours (unless you spend 1 hour repairing it to keep the device functioning), or when you use your action to dismantle it; at that time, you can reclaim the materials used to create it. You can have up to three such devices active at a time.

When you create a device, choose one of the following options:

Clockwork Toy: This toy is a clockwork animal, monster, or person, such as a frog, mouse, bird, dragon, or soldier. When placed on the ground, the toy moves 5 feet across the ground on each of your turns in a random direction. It makes noises as appropriate to the creature it represents.

Fire Starter: The device produces a miniature flame, which you can use to light a candle, torch, or campfire. Using the device requires your action.

Music Box: When opened, this music box plays a single song at a moderate volume. The box stops playing when it reaches the song's end or when it is closed.

features.name: Rock Gnome Trait: Kol points
features.desc:
You can spend these points to fuel various features. You start knowing three such features: Flurry of Blows, Patient Defense, and Step of the Wind. 

When you spend a kol point, it is unavailable until you finish a short or long rest, at the end of which you draw all of your expended kol back into yourself. You must spend at least 30 minutes of the rest meditating to regain your kol points.

Some of your kol features require your target to make a saving throw to resist the feature's effects. The saving throw DC is calculated as follows:

Kol save DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier
Beginning at 1st level, while you are wearing no armor and not wielding a shield, your AC equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Wisdom modifier.
features.name: Class Feature: Unarmored Defense

features.desc: You can use Dexterity instead of Strength for attack/damage rolls of unarmed strikes and monk weapons. You can roll a d4 in place of normal damage for these attacks. When you use an action to attack with a monk weapon or unarmed strike, you can make one unarmed strike as a bonus action.
features.name: Class Feature: Martial Arts

features.desc: Immediately after you take the Attack action on your turn, you can spend 1 kol point to make two unarmed strikes as a bonus action.
features.name: Class Feature: Flurry of Blows

features.desc: You can spend 1 kol point to take the Dodge action as a bonus action on your turn.
features.name: Class Feature: Patient Defense
Class Feature: Step of the Wind
features.desc: You can spend 1 kol point to take the Disengage or Dash action as a bonus action on your turn, and your jump distance is doubled for the turn.

companion.name: Little mouse
companion.desc:
The Ultimate Scout: Because it is a Tiny, ordinary creature, nobody pays attention to a mouse in a dungeon or a tavern. You can let it scurry under a door to see if there are guards on the other side, or drop it down a hole to see how deep it goes. Of course the challenge is, you can't speak mouse..

The Infiltrator: It can squeeze through iron bars, chew through very thin twine or paper, or knock over a glass on a table across the room to create a distraction so you can slip by.

inventory.desc: Homemade. As an action, you can spread a bag of caltrops to cover a square area that is 5 feet on a side.Any creature that enters the area must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or stop moving this turn and take 1 piercing damage.Taking this damage reduces the creature's walking speed by 10 feet until the creature regains at least 1 hit point.A creature moving through the area at half speed doesn't need to make the save.
inventory.name: Bag of Caltrops

inventory.desc: These special tools include the items needed to Tinker small objects and make minor repairs.
inventory.name: Tinker's Tools
Worn Overalls & Gnome cap
inventory.desc: Your standard Gnome outfit, commonly worn in Foarn.

inventory.name: Quarterstaff
inventory.desc: A simple sturdy wooden staff. Some Kolari carvings
Homemade ninja stars. Can we thrown to damage enemies from afar.
inventory.name: Gear-shaped ninja stars (Shuriken)
Thieves' Tools
inventory.desc: This set of tools includes a small file, a set of lock picks, a small mirror mounted on a metal handle, a set of narrow-bladed scissors, and a pair of pliers. Proficiency with these tools lets you add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make to disarm traps or open locks.

This pouch of cosmetics, hair dye, and small props lets you create disguises that change your physical appearance. Proficiency with this kit lets you add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make to create a visual disguise.
inventory.name: Disguise Kit

inventory.desc: Backpack, bedroll, mess kit, tinderbox, 10 torches, 10 days of rations, waterskin, 50 ft. hempen rope
inventory.name: Explorer's Pack
Flute
inventory.desc: If you have proficiency with a given musical instrument, you can add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make to play music with the instrument
A photo of Wendy's Mom, Dad and Uncle
inventory.name: Faded photo
Sketch of restaurant
inventory.desc: A sketch Wendy discovered of a beautiful restaurant by the sea

inventory.name: Loof's Loaf
inventory.desc: A loaf you received from Chauzy's baker Loof
A weapon you stole from Bengo the goblin
inventory.name: Scimitar

inventory.desc: A strange coin with a reptilian eye on it
inventory.name: Strange Coin

char_1775151236257.journal:
Pierre was delivered listening devices to monitor Tutel. The couriers were from the Crown. The crown is supposed to favor the tutels.
Lord pouti ship docked in bacillia. 
Old man ship at bacillia.
Leevy and chonko gnome detectives heading to bacillia looking for man with dagger who murdered chauzy merchant (Mikey)

Had dream. In middle of void. Heard voice speak. Looked up and saw reptile eye open. ‘Who are you.’ Its name was Clarion. It said I was special, sees greatness in me. 

Entrance of bacilia. Huge insurmountable wall. Crown paraphernalia all over. Guards posers of the crown. One guard one eye with eye patch recognizes my Lizzard belt. Has a tattoo of a stabbed lizzard on arm. Doesn’t like my kind. ‘Don’t pretend to be something you’re not. The Lacerta isn’t something to mess with’. Idk what lizzard represents yet. Should find that out

Spoke to peck a monk in bacilia. Was training two students. Took on a student and kicked ass. Gave me a ticket. He knows monk duku he grew up with him in Ustra. He follows the Humoolian way. Duku was interested in alll of the strengths of the 6 ways of monk (important). not really in the humoolian way. Kolari way is strength focused. Can have fists as strong as rocks. Humoolian more magic and moving things with mind. Hasn’t seen him in years but passes on a hello. 

Pavel (the real captain pavel) destroyed scorpion with us. Cool guy. Has like a million health. Crown armour. Got stabbed by scorpion and purple blotch happened.
The potion induced scorpion isn’t the first. Pav has been fighting all sorts of these anomalies. Part of old crown. Wants to clean up bacillia doesn’t like the bad things that happen here. Wants us to join him. 
Because Marc’s an idiot he has to appear before counselor Quesler for his looting

Lord Putys crab guy boat is the Shellhouse. He’s getting executed in two days. 

BACILIA PORT:
Super fancy. Tourist attraction. Royal guards. Fancy and beautiful. Portion of port with covered ports with bricks, less fancy. Kind of run down compared to the rest.

Scant did mikey dirty. ‘Deal that should have been clean’. Scant is running away from him. Hes looking for him. Royal guard doesn’t like scant. Traitor elf. Queslor used to use him for trades

Pavel has private room with broken crown on it. Man with matching robe with letter H on it sitting at bar. Mikey recognizes music being played. 

Basco a bird man in pavels private room just leaving as we arrived he saved Marc’s butt. Broke up a fight between him and bowen on a ship.

Potions are showing up, tizel sells them in his shop. He doesn’t even know what they do

Bacilia under the influence of pirates. Unfair trials happening. Pavel close friends with puty. Said he had an unfair trial. Not surprised he stole something from our mayor. Belong to ‘old crown’. Reject advice of newbies (haven tutel). Don’t like tutel family. Tutel and pirate jewl in kahoots. Jewl is aware of us, has counselor quesel under thumb. We need to rescue puty. Dead mayor under protection of old crown. Maybe crab man not bad. Mayor confused by putys actions
Puty is in basement of counselor Q mansion. Vault in basement. Prison alongside heavily guarded. Monitored to make sure nothing stops execution

Monk hayne trained Duku. Best monk to come from not form. Knows me. Duku was in bacilia a week ago. Set out on a ship. Doesn’t know who wandu is. Because it’s weird that they would have an outsider monk he is worried about Forn and heads there immediately. Welcomes me to come see him

Armand runs the Rusty hook in lower bacilia he can be trusted

Pavel does cool handshake with Ryland, he knows he’s a tutel.

Mikey talks to mouse Palch musician. They bond over magical music. ‘The stranger’ gave Mikey his powers. He doesn’t know how he got them. Wants to find him. Paid a high price/terms. Traded what was most charming about him for talent. Mouse gives Mikey lucky pocket watch. When he opens it an unfamiliar song plays. Tells Mikey to master the song, it will be more than a lucky pocket watch if he does. Stranger less visible these days. Mikey likes having sheet music. 
Palch asks Ryland if he’s seen him before. Ryland says no. Mouse smiles. He knows who he is too and is keeping his secret like Pavel.

Spent night at rusty hook. Had he same lizard eye dream. Mikey and Ryland went to gamble together

companion.name: Tiny Bear
companion.attacks:
Maul: The cub attacks with claws and teeth.

Entangling Growl (1/Short Rest): The cub lets out a sound mimicking the creaking of trees. One creature within 5 feet must succeed on a Strength Save (vs. your Spell Save DC) or be Restrained by spectral vines until the end of its next turn.
companion.traits: Primal Bond: You add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check or saving throw the beast makes.
companion.desc:
Primal Bond: You add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check or saving throw the beast makes.
Verdant Strike: Melee Weapon Attack: Your Spell Attack Modifier to hit, reach 5 ft. Damage: 1d6 + 2 + your Proficiency Bonus (Slashing). This damage becomes Force damage if the sickle is glowing.

Maul: The cub attacks with claws and teeth.
Entangling Growl (1/Short Rest): The cub lets out a sound mimicking the creaking of trees. One creature within 5 feet must succeed on a Strength Save (vs. your Spell Save DC) or be Restrained by spectral vines until the end of its next turn.

spells.name: Caroline's Laughter
spells.desc: Play a jaunty tune. One enemy makes a Wisdom save (DC 13). If they fail, they fall prone in fits of uncontrollable laughter, losing their turn.

You attempt to charm a humanoid you can see within range. It must make a wisdom saving throw, and does so with advantage if you or your companions are fighting it. If it fails the saving throw, it is charmed by you until the spell ends or until you or your companions do anything harmful to it. The charmed creature regards you as a friendly acquaintance. When the spell ends, the creature knows it was charmed by you.

At Higher Levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 1st. The creatures must be within 30 feet of each other when you target them.
spells.name: Charm Person
Sleep
spells.desc:
This spell sends creatures into a magical slumber. Roll 5d8; the total is how many hit points of creatures this spell can affect. Creatures within 20 feet of a point you choose within range are affected in ascending order of their current hit points (ignoring unconscious creatures).
Starting with the creature that has the lowest current hit points, each creature affected by this spell falls unconscious until the spell ends, the sleeper takes damage, or someone uses an action to shake or slap the sleeper awake. Subtract each creature's hit points from the total before moving on to the creature with the next lowest hit points. A creature's hit points must be equal to or less than the remaining total for that creature to be affected.
Undead and creatures immune to being charmed aren't affected by this spell.

At Higher Levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, roll an additional 2d8 for each slot level above 1st.
You create either a sound or an image of an object within 30 feet that lasts for 1 minute. The image can't be larger than a 5-foot cube. If it's a sound, it can range from a whisper to a scream.
spells.name: Minor Illusion
Earth Tremor
spells.desc: You cause a localized tremor. Every creature other than you within 10 feet must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, they take 1d6 bludgeoning damage and fall prone. Additionally, the ground in that 10-foot radius becomes difficult terrain.

spells.name: Faerie Fire
spells.desc:
Each object in a 20-foot cube within range is outlined in blue, green, or violet light (your choice). Any creature in the area when the spell is cast is also outlined in light if it fails a dexterity saving throw. For the duration, objects and affected creatures shed dim light in a 10-foot radius.
Any attack roll against an affected creature or object has advantage if the attacker can see it, and the affected creature or object can't benefit from being invisible.

You unleash a string of insults laced with subtle enchantments at a creature you can see within range. If the target can hear you (though it need not understand you), it must succeed on a wisdom saving throw or take 1d4 psychic damage and have disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn.
This spell's damage increases by 1d4 when you reach 5th level (2d4), 11th level (3d4), and 17th level (4d4).
spells.name: Vicious Mockery

char_1775351992469.name: Kehrfuffle Songroot
char_1775351992469.backstory:
Kehrfuffle grew up in the quiet village of Ivory Hollow, a beautiful town of Wood Elves that often saw visitors seeking tranquility. Kehrfuffle sought fame as an accordionist and often played at the local tavern. His charm brought many to his shows but he was often left dissatisfied feeling that he was not getting his big break as an artist, which he felt he deserved. Despite feeling disappointed by his own lack of fame, he found love in a beautiful wood elf named Caroline who truly loved his music and believed in him. She was beloved in Ivory Hollow, known for her sympathy for the worn down and weary. She had a beautiful singing voice which she was shy of sharing with anyone other than Kehrfuffle himself. While Kehrfuffle himself could draw a crowd, Caroline was even more immensely charming and it led to the two being a highly respected couple, even being appointed to the town council in Ivory Hollow. Kehrfuffle had a good life, but he failed to recognize that at the time, driven by his own ambition. 

Until a night that changed everything.

Finishing up a gig at the Root pub, a local tavern to Ivory Hollow, Kehrfuffle was beckoned to a dimly lit table at the back of the pub. There sat a hooded man who seemed small in stature. The man’s voice seemed like a whisper and yet every word hit Kehrfuffle’s ears clearly. He offered Kehrfuffle a deal: “Undeniable musical talent in exchange for the most charming thing he had.”
Kehrfuffle laughed it off thinking the man to be a strange fan of his, but on the off chance this mysterious stranger had a prestigious job to offer him Kehrfuffle was willing to accept, believing he was merely trading a physical object this man had his eye on. When his hand met the stranger’s in agreement he felt a grip he had never felt before. The man was instantly gone and Kehrfuffle was instantly transported back to his home. On the floor in front of him lay his wife’s apron next to a beautiful accordion. The accordion pulsing as if it were alive. The painful moments that followed led to the grave realisation that the man had taken the most charming thing about him, his beloved wife Caroline. Even now the accordion occasionally plays a sad song of its own, the notes humming in the key of his missing wife. Kehrfuffle proceeded to live for years fueled by anger and a desire for vengeance. He hunted the mysterious man, occasionally hearing talk of the shrouded man who appears where ambition and desperation meet, the same verse always recited:

“A lover desperate for a bride.”
“A warrior desperate for victory.”
“A singer desperate for fame.”

His search led him back to the ancient druidic ruins of the Weeping Grove, just outside Ivory Hollow. The wild magic of the grove reacted negatively to the presence of Caroline’s accordion. The shrine that had stood for years, an attraction drawing many passersby, collapsed, crushing Kehrfuffle’s left leg entirely. After coming to, he discovered his leg had been mysteriously replaced by a thick branch, being gnawed on by an unnaturally small bear cub. The cub has never left his side since. Additionally, a large and somewhat rusty Sicle lay in the dirt next to him, while holding the Sickle it feels as though Kerfuffle can hear the heartbeat or the vegetation around him and he notices a faint glow the symbols carved into the blade’s hilt. Taking this event as a sign from the druidic force Gesma whom the shrine had been made for, Kehrfuffle re-assessed his approach. While he works to increase in strength, he also looks for allies or heroes he can manipulate into helping him, alongside his quest for clues.

It has been a while since Kehrfuffle has heard talk of the shrouded stranger. While his approach may have changed, his goal remains the same and continues to consume him. Find the stranger. Bring Caroline back.

features.desc: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light.
features.name: Wood Elf Trait: Darkvision

features.desc: You have proficiency in the Perception skill.
features.name: Wood Elf Trait: Keen Senses

features.desc: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic can't put you to sleep.
features.name: Wood Elf Trait: Fey Ancestry
Wood Elf Trait: Elf Weapon Training
features.desc: You have proficiency with the longsword, shortsword, shortbow, and longbow.
Your base walking speed increases to 35 feet.
features.name: Wood Elf Trait: Fleet of Foot

features.desc: You can attempt to hide even when you are only lightly obscured by foliage, heavy rain, falling snow, mist, and other natural phenomena.
features.name: Wood Elf Trait: Mask of the Wild

features.desc: Elves do not need to sleep. Instead, they meditate deeply, remaining semiconscious, for 4 hours a day. (The Common word for such meditation is "trance.") While meditating, you can dream after a fashion; such dreams are actually mental exercises that have become reflexive through years of practice. After resting this way, you gain the same benefit that a human does from 8 hours of sleep.
features.name: Wood Elf Trait: Trance

features.desc: You have learned to untangle and reshape the fabric of reality in harmony with your wishes and music. Your spells are part of your vast repertoire, magic that you can tune to different situations.
features.name: Class Feature: Spellcasting: Bard

features.desc:
You can inspire others through stirring words or music. To do so, you use a bonus action on your turn to choose one creature other than yourself within 60 feet of you who can hear you. That creature gains one Bardic Inspiration die, a d6. Once within the next 10 minutes, the creature can roll the die and add the number rolled to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw it makes. The creature can wait until after it rolls the d20 before deciding to use the Bardic Inspiration die, but must decide before the GM says whether the roll succeeds or fails. Once the Bardic Inspiration die is rolled, it is lost. A creature can have only one Bardic Inspiration die at a time.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (a minimum of once). You regain any expended uses when you finish a long rest. 

Your Bardic Inspiration die changes when you reach certain levels in this class. The die becomes a d8 at 5th level, a d10 at 10th level, and a d12 at 15th level.
features.name: Class Feature: Bardic Inspiration (d6)

features.desc: You can always find a place to perform, usually in an inn or tavern. At such a place, you receive free lodging and food of a modest or comfortable standard as long as you perform each night.
features.name: Class Feature: By Popular Demand
Class Feature: Jack of All Trades
features.desc: Starting at 2nd level, you can add half your proficiency bonus, rounded down, to any ability check you make that doesn't already include your proficiency bonus.

Beginning at 2nd level, you can use soothing music or oration to help revitalize your wounded allies during a short rest. If you or any friendly creatures who can hear your performance regain hit points at the end of the short rest by spending one or more Hit Dice, each of those creatures regains an extra 1d6 hit points. 

The extra hit points increase when you reach certain levels in this class: to 1d8 at 9th level, to 1d10 at 13th level, and to 1d12 at 17th level.
features.name: Class Feature: Song of Rest (d6)

features.name: Variant Human Trait: Arrows
features.desc: Your current arrow count

features.name: Class Feature: Favored Foe
features.desc: When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can call on your mystical bond with nature to mark the target as your favored enemy for 1 minute or until you lose your concentration (as if concentrating on a spell). The first time on each of your turns that you hit the marked target and deal damage to it, including when you mark it, you deal an extra 1d4 damage. You can use this feature 2 times per long rest.

features.name: Class Feature: False Identity - Strider
features.desc: You have created a second identity that includes documentation, established acquaintances, and disguises that allow you to assume that persona. You can also forge documents if you have seen an example of the kind of document you are trying to copy.

You can choose to take a -5 penalty to the attack roll. If the attack hits, you add +10 to the damage (total damage: 1d8 + 13). You also ignore half and three-quarters cover, and attacking at long range doesn't impose disadvantage.
features.name: Class Feature: Sharpshooter

features.desc: While you are wearing armor, you gain a +1 bonus to AC.
features.name: Class Feature: Fighting Style: Defense

char_1775411940782.backstory:
Edmund Tudul is a royal, born to Lord Henry Tudul and Wendy Tudul of the crown. Edmund was the eldest of three children. 2 years the senior of his brother Haven. 5 years the senior of his younger sister Ruth. Edward loved his siblings very much. His brother Haven was much better in their combat classes despite being younger in years, Edward could only best him in Archery class. In time Edmund and Haven developed a bit of a rivalry, Edward proving to be a more compassionate and kind man and Haven proving to be more athletic and courageous. Their father never being in town for long, he and Haven often spent time with Emperor Hearn and Commander Goron listening to the two Limestone Goliath’s tell stories from the ancient war. Their imposing size yet kind manner left an immense impression on Edward. They taught him the value of having a good name with others, as the two commanding brothers were beloved to everyone in Auralis.

As he grew and trained in the court of the Crown, Prince Edmund began to develop a distaste for nobility. His access to the Emperor and Commander seemed to be restricted now, only catching sight of them from afar during imperial events. The Tudul family name didn’t carry admiration but instead carried fear. His father made attempts to connect with him, but Edward was disgusted by his family’s low moral compass and lack of compassion for the weak. As a result Edward refused to join company outings with his father, seeding his place to Haven who seemed to love the thrill of going on these trips.

On one occasion Edmund and his sister Princess Ruth were excited to welcome their brother home from a company trip. Seeking to surprise him the princess ran ahead of Edmund to sneak aboard the ship, her goal to scare Haven which always brought her great delight. Edmund waited by the shore. Soon everything would change.

A loud crash was heard on board the ship, followed by Haven’s disembarking. Haven seemed a shell of himself, making no response as his brother greeted him. Edmund was curious to his sister’s whereabouts and asked his brother, only to be met with a swift punch to the head that knocked him to the ground. Haven stood over him, merely repeating the words: “We can’t change what we are.” There Edmund lay for some time, shock racing through his mind. As he rose from the ground to his horror he saw his father disembarking from the ship, his sister Ruth’s limp body held in his arms. His father’s face showed no sign of grief as he tossed her body to the sea.

This grim memory continues to play out in Edmund’s mind. A story with many questions but no answers. Filled with grief at the loss of his beloved sister, and confusion at the circumstances that led to her death Edmund fled to Chauzy, leaving his royal life behind. There he knew his outcast uncle Byron Tudul would take him in. As the Mayor of Chauzy Byron Tudul seeks to redeem the Tudul name, an idea that Edward finds humorous. Edward instead rejects his nobility and the Tudul family name and now goes by the name Strider, his uncle agreeing to protect his identity. While he trains and pretends to be a nonchalant powerful Ranger he is still very much a spoiled prince, but he’s learning to fake it until it becomes his reality. If he truly learns to stop caring then maybe he’ll never have to face his brother or father again, and can make his own name in the world of Arkla. 

Fueled by the Emperor’s stories which he once held so dear, Strider seeks to have stories of his own so people will admire who he is now, and forget who he once was. In his mind Prince Edward Tudul died the same day that his sister Ruth did. He’ll make a name for himself that would've made her proud.

proficiencies.skills: Deception (Expertise), History, Stealth, Perception, Insight, Persuasion,

char_1775411940782.name: Edmund "Strider" Tudul

spells.name: Hunter's Mark
spells.desc: You choose a creature you can see within range and mystically mark it as your quarry. Until the spell ends, you deal an extra 1d6 damage to the target whenever you hit it with a weapon attack, and you have advantage on any Wisdom (Perception) or Wisdom (Survival) check you make to find it. If the target drops to 0 hit points before this spell ends, you can use a bonus action on a subsequent turn of yours to mark a new creature.

Up to ten berries appear in your hand and are infused with magic for the duration. A creature can use its action to eat one berry. Eating a berry restores 1 hit point, and the berry provides enough nourishment to sustain a creature for a day.
The berries lose their potency if they have not been consumed within 24 hours of the casting of this spell.
spells.name: Goodberry

spells.desc: You make a ranged spell attack (+5 to hit) against one target. If it hits, the target takes 1d10 fire damage. A flammable object hit by this spell ignites if it isn't being worn or carried.

This spell repairs a single break or tear in an object you touch, such as a broken key, a torn cloak, or a leaking wineskin. As long as the break or tear is no longer than 1 foot in any dimension, you mend it, leaving no trace of the former damage.

char_1775502189533.backstory:
Toern loves explosives. His entire life revolved around experiments and gadgets, much to the chagrin of his wizard family. The Treetap family line has long been revered in not only Dagend but all of Arkla as a family of powerful wizards. At a young age though Toern realized this was not the path for him. While his brothers Tulip and Tomper mastered powerful elemental magic, Toern preferred to spend his time harnessing magic into other things, at first experimenting with putting it inside various pond creatures from the local Markel Bog. His experimentation often ended in some sort of explosion, highly disruptive to the amphibian wildlife.

Despite not fitting in, Toern loved his family deeply and felt their support as he continued to tinker with explosives. He quickly became adept at making powerful mining bombs, protective magical mines and imbuing magic into various little gadgets and inventions. These were distributed far and wide throughout Dagend and contributed greatly to the prosperous nature of a somewhat pretentious and affluent city, stemming from its successful mines and trade agreement with the Crown.

One day, while working on a bomb that he claimed would instantaneously clear a tunnel without bringing any harm to miners, precious jewels or mineral veins, Toern’s life changed completely. The bomb he was proudly unveiling to the world went off. The result was a real mess, his family, friends and many local residents of Dagend were blown into magical goo. Where they once stood was only a pile of gelatinous matter. Toern was convinced he had built the bomb well, as he always did. A perfectionist, he had checked it over many times. How could this have happened?

In his grief, while collecting the goo of his loved ones into an assortment of jars, to pay his respects, he noticed something truly mysterious, his elder brother Tulin’s goo was gone. Where his powerful wizard brother had one stood, right at Toern’s side, was merely a singed patch of grass and tucked into the dirt was a metal pin, the pin featured two lizards in the shape of two Ts and was far too tacky for his brother’s taste. 

Armed with hope that his powerful brother had somehow survived this horrible accident and with ambition to redeem himself as a successful inventor and not a crazed scientist, Toern set out to sea with a crew of Gnome friends in search of answers. The crew quickly crowned Toern their captain after his bombs aided them in dissuading many pirate attacks and other ocean threats. His first mate Bolan, a powerful quartz-stone Goliath quickly became Toern’s dearest friend and together the two commanded their Gnome crew successfully for a while, though they often faced conflict as Bolan wanted them to turn to piracy while Toern’s moral compass kept him strictly working under the Crown’s flag.

Now Toern realizes how significant that conflict really was. One night he was rudely awoken by Bolan, a strange look on his face. Bolan bound Toern and threw him overboard. The crew had mutinized against him. That day was the birth of the Quartz pirates. At the brink of drowning the ocean around Toern began to glow. Suddenly it felt as though a giant hand ripped him from the ocean and plopped him on the deck of Captain Screwbeard, a fat and stupid Dwarf admiral of the Jewl pirates. Screwbeard kept Toern locked up. After hearing grumblings of a new letter from the boss, and a mysterious clue to a weapon held by the Mayor of Chauzy, Toern has been relocated to Poll cave where he is currently held, under lock and key by Screwbeard, now apparently the first mate of the Jewl pirates, and his goblin underlings.

proficiencies.tools: Tinker's Tools, Thieves' Tools, Alchemist's Supplies, Navigator's Tools, Water Vehicles,

char_1775502189533.name: Toern Treetap

features.name: Salt Gnome Trait: Darkvision
features.desc: You have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You cannot discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.
You have advantage on all Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws against magic.
features.name: Salt Gnome Trait: Gnome Cunning
Salt Gnome Trait: Saltwater Heritage
features.desc: You have a swimming speed of 25 feet and resistance to Cold damage.

features.name: Class Feature: Magical Tinkering
features.desc:
You've learned how to invest a spark of magic into mundane objects. To use this ability, you must have thieves' tools or artisan's tools in hand. You then touch a Tiny nonmagical object as an action and give it one of the following magical properties of your choice: 

- The object sheds bright light in a 5-foot radius and dim light for an additional 5 feet.
- Whenever tapped by a creature, the object emits a recorded message that can be heard up to 10 feet away.
- The object continuously emits your choice of an odor or a nonverbal sound.
- A static visual effect appears on one of the object's surfaces.

features.name: Class Feature: Spellcasting (Artificer)
features.desc: You have studied the workings of magic and how to channel it through objects. As a result, you have gained the ability to cast spells. To observers, you don't appear to be casting spells in a conventional way; you look as if you're producing wonders using mundane items or outlandish inventions.

features.name: Class Feature: Infuse Item
features.desc:
You have the ability to imbue mundane items with certain magical infusions, turning them into magic items.

Known Infusions: 4

Max Active Infusions: 2

Mechanics: Whenever you finish a long rest, you can touch a nonmagical object and imbue it with one of your known artificer infusions. Your magic lasts indefinitely, but if you die, the infusion vanishes after a number of days equal to your Intelligence modifier.

The Rules: An object can bear only one infusion at a time. You can only infuse one object with a specific infusion at a time (no duplicates). If you try to exceed your maximum number of active infusions (2), the oldest active infusion immediately ends.

Enhanced Defense: You infuse a suit of armor or a shield. The wearer gains a +1 bonus to their Armor Class. 

Repeating Shot: You infuse a simple or martial weapon with the ammunition property (like a bow or crossbow). It grants a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls. Furthermore, it magically generates its own ammunition instantly when fired and ignores the loading property. 

Replicate Magic Item (Alchemy Jug): You replicate a specific magic item. An Alchemy Jug can magically produce varying amounts of different liquids on command, including fresh water, acid, basic poison, oil, or even mayonnaise. (Incredibly thematic for a salt gnome who loves chucking alchemical vials).

Homunculus Servant: You infuse a gem or crystal worth at least 100 gp to create a tiny, flying mechanical creature. It obeys your commands, shares your initiative in combat, and you can use your bonus action to command it to attack or assist. (You get to design what it looks like—perhaps a little clockwork seagull or lizard!)

entityData.traits:
Dim-Witted Commander: Screwbeard thinks he is a brilliant tactician. At the start of his turn, he shouts a confusing order. Roll a d4. On a 1, one random goblin is confused and loses their movement speed this turn.

Top-Heavy: Because of his massive belly and ill-fitting armor, Screwbeard has disadvantage on Dexterity (Acrobatics) checks to maintain his balance on the slippery saltstone near the water.

spells.name: Fireball
spells.desc:
A bright streak flashes from your pointing finger to a point you choose within range and then blossoms with a low roar into an explosion of flame. Each creature in a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on that point must make a dexterity saving throw. A target takes 8d6 fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
The fire spreads around corners. It ignites flammable objects in the area that aren't being worn or carried.

At Higher Levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 3rd.
